To find the characteristic polynomial of the given matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $I$ is the $4 \times 4$ identity matrix and $\lambda$ is the scalar variable.

The matrix $A$ is:
$$
A = \left(\begin{array}{cccc}
 \frac{1}{2} & -\frac{101}{8} & -\frac{17}{8} & -\frac{47}{4} \\
 -\frac{75}{8} & -\frac{109}{8} & \frac{111}{8} & -\frac{9}{4} \\
 \frac{27}{8} & \frac{19}{8} & -\frac{21}{2} & -\frac{3}{4} \\
 \frac{35}{4} & \frac{13}{8} & -\frac{21}{2} & \frac{25}{8}
\end{array}\right)
$$

**Step 1: Simplify the matrix to avoid fractions.**
The denominators are powers of 2 (2, 4, 8). The least common multiple is 8. Let's factor out $\frac{1}{8}$ from the entire matrix.
$$
A = \frac{1}{8} \left(\begin{array}{cccc}
 4 & -101 & -17 & -94 \\
 -75 & -109 & 111 & -18 \\
 27 & 19 & -84 & -6 \\
 70 & 13 & -84 & 25
\end{array}\right)
$$
